etymology.description: šĺĆ (ancient form) used phonetically in ťôį
etymology.interpretation: The character šĺĆ (šĺĆ) (yńęn, "phonetic") serves as a phonetic component in the compound ťôį(ťôį) (yńęn, "shade; feminine principle") when combined with the semantic ťėú(ťėú) (f√Ļ, "mound; hill"). It's an ancient form primarily contributing to the phonetic aspect of ťôį.
